# Final Review Aggregate

## Accepted

- `fr-3-7c2e7fc3`

## Rejected

None.

## Disputed

None.

## Final Accepted Amendments

### `fr-3-7c2e7fc3`

Finding: `task show`/`task select` now reject any project without `[task_source]` (`src/cli/task.rs:106-119`, `src/cli/task.rs:155-166`) and `task list` now filters those projects out entirely (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:1399-1422`). That changes the `task` namespace from the alias/wrapper behavior the product still advertises in `src/cli/project.rs:78-85` into a task-source-only view. The regression is user-visible today: a standalone project disappears from `task list`, and `task show <id>` / `task select <id>` fail with `ProjectNotTask` even though `project --help` tells operators to use those commands instead. It also misclassifies legacy bead-backed projects that are still recovered elsewhere via lineage fallback (`src/cli/project.rs:803-829`, `src/cli/project.rs:3881-3946`), because `is_milestone_task()` only checks explicit `task_source`. The rewritten assertions in `tests/cli.rs:19443-19457` and `tests/cli.rs:19628-19684` lock this regression in. Remediation: keep this bead to help-text updates only and revert the new filtering/gating, or preserve the original wrapper semantics plus legacy-lineage detection before tightening task-only behavior.
